scope_boundary: Artifact Graph record-reality sequence bridge only. The fixture accepts one finite stale/partial/fresh replay sequence and rejects four expected-invalid controls for stale-certificate support movement, restoration without fresh replay/provenance, missing non-claim boundaries, and support review without replay-validated transaction state. It is not a deployed artifact graph, not a deployed replay engine, not deployed audit behavior, not open-world receipt faithfulness, not verifier-correctness evidence, not source-interpretation evidence, and not an upward support-state transition.
transition_reason: The record-reality sequence bridge is useful because it makes a key Artifact Graph distinction executable: a record can lose bounded-review eligibility when a certificate is stale or replay is partial, and it can regain bounded-review eligibility only after a fresh byte-exact replay/provenance event with replay-validated transaction state, digest verification, independent checking, non-claim boundaries, and no support-state effect. The evidence remains a finite local fixture, so this accepted decision blocks promotion for deployed artifact-graph behavior, deployed replay, deployed audit durability, open-world receipt faithfulness, verifier correctness, provenance completeness, source interpretation, benchmark performance, model quality, safety, ASI, or the Artifact Graphs chapter core claim.
required_artifacts: deployed or externally reviewable artifact-graph replay trace
public-safe produced artifact with parent job, source/context set, context transaction, semantic certificate, replay declaration, residual declaration, evidence gate, and claim/test link
fresh replay/provenance event over real or externally replayed artifacts
independent observation route outside the producing component
trap receipt or challenge suite executed outside the producing component
deployed or externally reviewable audit durability evidence
source-interpretation adequacy review where source-derived claims are involved
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: claimed restoration without fresh replay/provenance is rejected
support review without replay-validated transaction state is rejected
fresh replay restoration remains bounded to the finite fixture
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: Artifact graph record-reality sequence validator failure
stale certificate, incomplete replay, missing non-claim boundary, support-state effect, or replay-unvalidated support review accepted as valid

fresh replay restoration described as deployed artifact-graph behavior, deployed replay, audit durability, open-world receipt faithfulness, verifier correctness, source interpretation, benchmark, model-quality, safety, ASI, or chapter-core evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed Artifact Graph record-reality claim after deployed or externally reviewable replay traces, produced-artifact provenance records, independent observer routes, trap/challenge execution outside the producing component, verifier-quality evidence, provenance-completeness review, audit-durability evidence, source-interpretation review where applicable, residuals, non-claims, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.
limitations: one valid synthetic stale/partial/fresh replay sequence only
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not promote the Artifact Graphs chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent replay, audit, verifier-quality, source-interpretation, external-project, or external human review

no independent observation route outside the producing component
